mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources through custom resources. It is ideal for users who prefer GitOps workflows or infrastructure as code practices. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRDs and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command sets up the Grafana Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator grafana/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource (CR) for Grafana, which defines the configuration for your Grafana instance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

kubectl get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=grafana-operator

```

> Displays the details of the Grafana instance.
> Lists the pods created by the Grafana Operator.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Removes the Grafana Operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the Grafana instance created earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This step involves upgrading the operator and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana

```

> Backs up the current Grafana configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator pod using 'kubectl logs <grafana-operator-pod-name>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to reconcile Grafana instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be a misconfiguration in the Grafana CR.

**Fix:**
Review the Grafana CR configuration and ensure all required fields are set correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana dashboard not loading
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not be fully initialized.

**Fix:**
Wait for a few minutes and check the status of the Grafana instance again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to datasource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The datasource configuration may be incorrect or the datasource may not be available.

**Fix:**
Verify the datasource configuration in the Grafana CR and ensure the datasource is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' and the Grafana dashboard accessible via the specified service.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
